parsed.clarification_question: By 'make this stronger', do you mean to enhance the conceptual and argumentative aspects (e.g., thesis, logic, evidence, novelty), the presentation aspects (e.g., structure, writing style), or both?

rationale.R1.1: The vague delegation to make the text stronger likely includes reframing the thesis and contribution claims to increase their impact and persuasiveness.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the text involves verifying and improving logical consistency in the argument chain to make the overall narrative more robust.
rationale.R1.3: Aligning claims with reported evidence is central to making the research presentation stronger and more credible.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing and emphasizing novelty against prior work can help strengthen the contribution's perceived value.
rationale.R1.5: Reorganizing the structure at section or subsection levels could enhance the narrative arc, making the text stronger overall.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence- and paragraph-level writing is expected to make the text more concise, flowing, and impactful.
rationale.R1.7: Improving citation accuracy and literature coverage can bolster the scholarly foundation, contributing to a stronger paper draft.
rationale.RX.1: Disclosing uncertainties is a central responsibility when strengthening claims to maintain transparency in research writing.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is load-bearing to ensure that strengthening the text does not lead to unsubstantiated assertions.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the scope of the original artifact is expected when making improvements to prevent mission creep.
rationale.RX.4: Considering downstream harms is peripheral here, as the artifact on text-to-SQL evaluation debiasing appears low-risk.
rationale.RX.5: Ensuring provenance and traceability of referenced benchmarks and models is central for responsible research communication.
